name: Batch insert and verify with SQLite
description: Tests that the agent uses sqlwrite for batch insert and verifies by reading data back
prompt: Write MATLAB code to insert a table of 5 sensor readings (columns: SensorID, Timestamp, Value) into a SQLite database table called 'readings'. Then verify the insert by reading the data back.

assertions.description: Uses sqlwrite for the batch insert (not row-by-row loop)
Creates a MATLAB table with correct column names before inserting
Verifies the insert by reading data back with sqlread

name: Atomic multi-table insert with transaction control
description: Tests that the agent uses proper transaction control for atomic multi-table writes
prompt: Write MATLAB code to atomically update two database tables: insert a new order into 'orders' and insert its line items into 'order_items'. If either insert fails, roll back both. Use transaction control.

assertions.description: Sets conn.AutoCommit to 'off' before the transaction
Uses try/catch block wrapping both sqlwrite calls
Calls commit(conn) in the try block after both writes succeed
Calls rollback(conn) in the catch block
Restores AutoCommit to 'on' after the transaction block
Does NOT write each table independently without transaction protection
assertions.expected: Both sqlwrite calls inside try block

name: Chunked bulk insert for large data
description: Tests that the agent chunks large data for sqlwrite instead of inserting row-by-row
prompt: I have a MATLAB table with 50,000 rows of results. Write code to insert them into a database table called 'results' efficiently, handling potential memory issues.

assertions.description: Does NOT insert rows one at a time in a loop
assertions.expected: No sqlwrite(conn, ..., data(i,:)) in a per-row loop

assertions.description: Implements a chunked loop (splits data into slices and calls sqlwrite on each slice)
assertions.expected: Loop with chunkSize and sqlwrite on data(startIdx:endIdx, :)

name: Update rows with sqlupdate and rowfilter
description: Tests that the agent uses the modern sqlupdate with rowfilter approach
prompt: Update rows in the 'products' table where Category is 'Electronics' to set Discount to 0.15. Use the modern approach.

assertions.description: Uses sqlupdate (not raw SQL UPDATE via execute)
Uses rowfilter to specify the Category == 'Electronics' condition
Creates a data table with the Discount value to apply
